markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked GTA VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch following two delays that pushed it from a 2025 window, giving traders roughly five months of runway for events in the “What will happen before GTA VI?” market. Summer 2026 marketing is the key catalyst, with Take-Two signaling that pre-orders and likely Trailer 3 will arrive as the campaign intensifies in late June. High anticipation, combined with Rockstar’s reputation for polished open-world releases, keeps attention on both industry milestones like new album drops or chart debuts and unrelated news such as geopolitical developments. The extended timeline heightens uncertainty around which pop-culture or global events will resolve before launch, as traders weigh momentum from confirmed marketing windows against the game’s history of last-minute refinements.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Harvey Weinstein prison time?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment heavily favors "No Prison Time" at 80% implied probability** because Weinstein's ongoing New York retrial process has repeatedly produced mistrials, most recently in May 2026 on the third-degree rape charge involving Jessica Mann, while his California 16-year sentence faces active appeals that could reduce or eliminate consecutive time. Multiple hung juries and prosecutorial decisions on whether to pursue a fourth Manhattan trial signal evidentiary and witness challenges that complicate securing additional convictions. Weinstein, now 74 and reporting serious health issues while held at Rikers, has explored plea options that could run any new time concurrently or result in release on time served. The California conviction remains under appeal after a skeptical April 2026 hearing, creating realistic pathways for reduced overall incarceration. Upcoming catalysts include the June 24 status hearing on further prosecution and appellate rulings on the Los Angeles case, which continue to shape market views on whether he faces meaningful additional prison exposure beyond what he has already served.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights sits tied 2-2 after Carolina’s 5-3 Game 4 road win on June 9, driven by strong first-period play and contributions from forwards like Jordan Staal. Carolina enters Game 5 at home on June 11 holding a modest implied-probability edge, reflecting its Eastern Conference-leading regular-season form, depth across lines, and ability to win in hostile environments during the playoffs. Vegas counters with proven postseason experience, a potent attack that has kept games high-scoring, and road resilience shown earlier in the series. Recent momentum shifts, special-teams execution, and home-ice advantage in the remaining games continue to shape trader consensus around these two contenders.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81.8% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Champion market primarily because they lead the San Antonio Spurs 3-1 in the NBA Finals and need just one more victory in the best-of-seven series. New York’s dramatic 107-106 Game 4 comeback from a 29-point third-quarter deficit at Madison Square Garden has reinforced trader confidence in their resilience and closing ability after earlier series wins. The Spurs captured Game 3 to extend the matchup but have struggled to regain consistent home-court momentum following earlier playoff successes that included a Western Conference Finals upset. With the series shifting back to San Antonio for a potential Game 5, the current pricing reflects the Knicks’ strong position while acknowledging the realistic upset window still available to a Spurs roster featuring standout talent.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain lead implied probabilities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after capturing Euro 2024 and maintaining strong recent form behind a young core including Lamine Yamal. France sit just behind with exceptional squad depth and back-to-back final appearances, while England’s consistent European showings and attacking options place them in the next tier alongside defending champions Argentina. Portugal, Brazil, and Germany follow, sustained by star quality and historical pedigree. With all major contenders qualified and squads finalized just days before kickoff, the bunched pricing reflects balanced assessments of current form, roster strength, and the absence of any dominant pre-tournament frontrunner.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party and its military apparatus as of mid-2026, with recent developments reinforcing rather than undermining his position. Extensive purges of senior PLA officers, including Central Military Commission vice chairman Zhang Youxia and Joint Staff Department chief Liu Zhenli in January 2026, along with earlier removals of other CMC members, have been interpreted by analysts as efforts to enforce loyalty and centralize authority ahead of the 21st Party Congress. No credible successor has been elevated, internal factional challenges remain absent from official channels, and Xi continues routine public and diplomatic activities, including a June 2026 visit to North Korea. These factors underpin trader consensus that removal before the end of 2026 is unlikely, consistent with historical patterns of incumbency durability in the absence of visible elite fractures.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.4% amid a fragmented field more than a year after the 2024 election, reflecting his high national profile as California governor, fundraising capacity, and early positioning in key states. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, supported by progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility respectively. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, buoyed by swing-state records and party infrastructure. Trader consensus highlights name recognition, primary-state polling trends, and potential 2026 midterm performance as key differentiators. Consolidation could occur through endorsements from party leaders, strong debate showings, or shifts in early-state voter coalitions before formal 2027-2028 primary contests begin.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.4% amid a fragmented field more than a year after the 2024 election, reflecting his high national profile as California governor, fundraising capacity, and early positioning in key states. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, supported by progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility respectively. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, buoyed by swing-state records and party infrastructure. Trader consensus highlights name recognition, primary-state polling trends, and potential 2026 midterm performance as key differentiators. Consolidation could occur through endorsements from party leaders, strong debate showings, or shifts in early-state voter coalitions before formal 2027-2028 primary contests begin.
